sections.summary: Non‑substantive heading indicating the document is the Standard for Table Olives.
Document identifier: Codex Standard CXS 66-1981.

sections.section: Adopted in 1981. Revised in 1987, 2013. Amended in 2022, 2023.
sections.summary: Revision history: adopted 1981; revised 1987 and 2013; amended 2022 and 2023.
Amendment notice: following CAC‑45 (Dec 2022), amendments were made to Section 8.2 (labelling of non‑retail containers).
Amendment notice: following CAC‑46 (Dec 2023), amendments were made to Section 9 (methods of analysis and sampling).

Scope: applies to suitably treated or processed fruit of Olea europaea L. offered for direct consumption as table olives (including catering and bulk containers intended for repacking) and excludes products indicated as intended for further processing.

Overview: defines table olives and sets out classification by ripeness and trade preparations/treatments, varietal suitability, presentation styles, and rules for other presentations and naming to avoid consumer confusion.

Defines 'table olives' as products from sound Olea europaea fruits selected for processing, debittered and preserved by natural fermentation, heat treatment or other means (with or without preservatives) to ensure stability, and packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium in accordance with Section 3.1.3.
States that table olives are classified by olive type (ripeness) and by trade preparations/treatments.

Classifies olive types by ripeness: green (harvested before colouring at normal size), olives turning colour (harvested at colour change before full ripeness) and black olives (harvested fully ripe or slightly before full ripeness).

Specifies trade preparations: 'treated' (alkaline‑treated) and 'natural' (placed directly in brine for partial or complete fermentation, with or without acidifying agents), dehydrated/shrivelled types (preserved in brine or by dry salt/heating/other processes), and olives darkened by oxidation which must be preserved in hermetically‑sealed containers and heat‑sterilized; allows 'specialities' prepared by other means provided the fruit meets the general definitions and the product name clearly prevents consumer confusion.
Varietal types: any commercially cultivated olive variety (cultivar) suitable for processing is permitted.

Lists permissible presentation styles (whole, cracked, split; stoned/pitted including halved, quartered, divided, sliced, chopped/minced, broken; stuffed; salad olives; and olives with capers or medleys) and defines criteria and naming requirements for these styles.
Defines whole olives (natural shape, stone intact), cracked olives (flesh opened without breaking the stone) and split olives (lengthwise cut into the skin and part of the flesh).

Defines stoned/pitted olives and substyles: stoned (stone removed, natural shape retained), halved, quartered, divided, sliced, chopped/minced (small pieces with no more than 5 per 100 by weight of identifiable stem‑insertion units or slice fragments), and broken olives (may include pieces of stuffing material).
Defines stoned (pitted) olives as olives from which the stone (pit) has been removed while basically retaining their natural shape.
Defines halved olives as stoned (pitted) or stuffed olives sliced into two approximately equal parts perpendicular to the longitudinal axis.
Defines quartered olives as stoned (pitted) olives split into four approximately equal parts along and perpendicular to the major axis of the fruit.
Defines divided olives as stoned (pitted) olives cut lengthwise into more than four approximately equal parts.
Defines sliced olives as stoned (pitted) or stuffed olives sliced into segments of fairly uniform thickness.

Defines chopped or minced olives as small pieces of stoned (pitted) olives of no definite shape that are practically devoid (no more than 5 per 100 units by weight) of identifiable stem-insertion units and slice fragments.
Defines broken olives as olives broken while being stoned (pitted) or stuffed, which may contain pieces of the stuffing material.

Defines stuffed olives as stoned (pitted) olives filled with one or more suitable products (e.g., pimiento, onion, almond, celery, anchovy, olive, citrus peel, hazelnut, capers) or with edible pastes.
Defines salad olives as whole, broken or broken-and-stoned olives with or without capers plus stuffing material, where olives are the most numerous component of the marketed product in this style.

Defines olives with capers or medley as whole or stoned (usually small) olives combined with capers and optionally stuffing, packed with other pickled edible products (e.g., pieces of onion, carrot, celery, pepper) where olives are the predominant ingredient.

Permits other presentations of the product provided they are sufficiently distinctive from prescribed styles, meet all relevant Standard requirements (including limits on defects, drained weight and applicable style requirements), and are adequately described on the label so as not to confuse or mislead the consumer.
Heading introducing the essential composition and quality factors for table olives; no substantive provisions in this heading itself.
Heading only: '3.1 Composition' introducing the composition subsection for table olives.
States the basic ingredients are olives as defined in Sections 1 and 2, with or without a liquid packing medium.

Lists permitted additional ingredients for table olives, including water; food-grade salts; vinegar; olive oil or other edible vegetable oils; sugars or sweetening foods (e.g., honey); edible accompaniments or stuffing materials (e.g., pimiento, onion, almond, celery, anchovy, capers or their pastes); and spices, aromatic herbs or their natural extracts.

Defines packing media (brines) as solutions of food-grade salts in potable water with optional permitted ingredients, requires brine to be clean and comply with hygiene rules in Section 6 and notes fermented olives may contain fermentation microorganisms; it also sets physico‑chemical limits in Table 1 (e.g., minimum NaCl: treated olives 5.0%, natural olives 6.0%, dehydrated/shrivelled 8.0%; maximum pH generally 4.3 or GMP where indicated) and states non‑complying trade preparations should be appropriately processed to meet food safety recommendations.

Requires table olives to have normal colour, flavour, odour and texture characteristic of the finished product and for olives and brine to be free from microbiological deterioration and extraneous tastes or smells, and specifies that whole, stoned (pitted) and stuffed styles must meet the minimum quality requirements in the second category of Table 3 while other styles must meet Table 4.
Heading introducing optional trade classification of table olives with designated categories if the product is classified.

Defines the 'Extra' (Fancy/A) category as the highest quality olives maximally endowed with variety and preparation characteristics, allowing only very slight colour, shape, flesh‑firmness or skin defects that do not affect overall appearance or organoleptic qualities; applicable to whole, split, stoned (pitted) and stuffed olives of suitable varieties.

Defines the 'First' (1st/Choice/Select/B) category as good quality olives with suitable ripeness and varietal/preparation characteristics that may have slight defects without affecting overall aspect, and permits all types, preparations and styles except chopped or broken olives.

Defines the 'Second' (2nd/Standard/C) category as olives of acceptable quality that do not qualify for the two higher categories but comply with the general conditions for table olives set out in this section.

Requires table olives to be uniform in size and, if size‑graded, to use the count-per-kilogramme scale (Table 2) applicable to whole, stoned (pitted) and stuffed styles; for checking, after removing the largest and smallest from a 100-olive sample the remaining olives' horizontal diameters must differ by no more than 4 mm, with alternative over/under size tolerances of −10%, −5% or −2% depending on the size interval.

Provides definitions and thresholds for defects including harmless extraneous vegetable matter; blemished fruit (>9 mm² skin marks); mutilated, broken and shrivelled fruit; abnormal texture and colour; stems (defect if >3 mm from shoulder except when whole olives are presented with stem attached); defective stuffing; stone/pit fragments (>2 mm longest axis); 'soft' (lacking characteristic firmness) and 'excessively soft' (spongy/watery, disintegrated flesh, or when the pit can be felt under moderate pressure).

Sets maximum defect tolerances by trade category and type/style in Table 3 (whole, stoned or stuffed olives) and Table 4 (broken, chopped, minced, sliced and other segmented styles), giving specific limits for items such as stones, broken fruit and blemishes; these tolerances are to be assessed in a minimum sample with specified sampling plans. The tolerances for whole/stoned/stuffed olives are assessed on a minimum sample of 200 olives (AQL 6.5) and those for segmented styles on a minimum 300 g sample (AQL 6.5).

Recommends that products covered by this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant Codex Codes of Hygienic Practice (e.g., for low/acidified canned foods and canned fruit/vegetable products) and other applicable Codex texts.
Section heading for Weights and Measures; no substantive provisions in this section text.
Section heading for Fill of container; no substantive provisions in this section text.

Requires the container (product plus packing medium) to occupy at least 90% of the container's water capacity (allowing necessary headspace per good manufacturing practices) and defines water capacity as the volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container holds when completely filled.
Defines any container failing to meet the minimum fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 as a 'defective'.
Specifies lot acceptance for fill: a lot meets Section 7.1.1 when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) from the applicable sampling plan using an AQL of 6.5.

Sets minimum net drained weight requirements for olives calculated relative to the container's water capacity (distilled water at 20°C): whole olives minimum 50% and stoned (pitted) and stuffed olives minimum 40%.

Establishes tolerance limits for net drained weight by container drained-weight ranges—<200 g: 5%; 200–500 g: 4%; 500–1,500 g: 3%; >1,500 g: 2%—applicable provided the sample's mean net drained weight is equal to or exceeds the declared weight, and any container outside these tolerances is a 'defective'.

Provides lot acceptance for drained weight: compliance when the average drained weight of examined containers is at least the required minimum and the number of 'defectives' (per Section 7.1.4 tolerances) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.
Section heading for Labelling; no substantive provisions in this section text.
States that retail containers must be labelled per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and that additional specific provisions in this standard also apply.

Requires the product name to be 'olives' or 'table olives' and to be accompanied (or in close proximity) by the type, trade preparation, style (with stuffing specified for stuffed olives), and optionally size, trade category and variety, with some declarations allowed to be omitted on transparent packs or glass jars and additional wording required to avoid consumer confusion for other styles.
Directs that labelling of non-retail containers follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Section heading for Methods of Analysis and Sampling; no substantive provisions beyond the heading.
Mandates that methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard shall be those contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) for checking compliance.

sections.section: 9.1.1 For  checking  the  compliance  with  this  standard,  the  methods  of  analysis  and  sampling  contained  in  the Recommended  Methods  of  Analysis  and  Sampling (CXS  234-1999) 14   relevant  to  the provisions  in  this standard, shall be used.
sections.summary: Reiterates that the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard shall be used for compliance checking.
States that methods of sampling are described in the standard (no further detail provided in this text).
Fragmentary text only: 'As described in the standard.' with no substantive content.
